Recommendation / Requirement: An SBOM system that follows the guidance and framing proposed in this document must support these Baseline Attributes.
An SBOM system or format may support supplemental Attributes.
The Author Name Attribute should name as many participants involved in authoring the SBOM data as possible.
The tool(s) used to create the SBOM can also be declared to assist in SBOM data consumption.
An SBOM must list the entity that prompted the creation of the SBOM.
The Author Name Attribute should include the name of the legal entity and some form of unique identification (e.g., an email address or website) if possible.
If no legal entity name is available, attempt to uniquely identify the SBOM creator along with contact information.
Code: Identify SBOM creator if no entity

Recommendation / Requirement: In addition to listing the entity that prompted the creation of the SBOM, identify tool(s) and version(s) that assisted the Author in the SBOMs creation.
As a minimum expectation, the Timestamp should be consistent across time zones and locales and use a common international format, such as ISO 8601 (e.g., 2024-05-23T13:51:37Z).
This Attribute is optional and considered an aspirational goal.
For each Component in the SBOM, identifying each Attribute should be attempted as indicated in the data maturity levels.
Code: Populate all component attributes adhering to Maturity Levels

Recommendation / Requirement: An SBOM needs to capture transitive, or nested, Supply Chain relationships between Components to the extent that these dependency relationships are known.
SBOMs are expected to identify all static, direct Dependencies of the root or primary Component.
In addition to the direct Dependencies, SBOMs should identify as many levels of subcomponents as possible.
In addition to the direct Dependencies and subcomponents identified in the SBOM, efforts are made to uniquely and unambiguously identify Dependencies that are dynamic and/or remote.
Formats and tooling need to provide the capability to handle multiple names or aliases.
As a minimum expectation, the Component name should declare the commonly used public name for the Component.
This Attribute helps to further identify a Component and should be separate from the Component Name.
Recording what is provided from the Supplier accurately is the primary goal.
Semantic versioning is preferred.
If the Component does not have a unique semantic version available to declare, make sure that a cryptographic hash is provided for the Component.
As a minimum expectation, declare the version string as provided by the Supplier.
The Supplier Name should be identified carefully as it is a significant contributor to achieving Component identification at scale.
The Supplier Name should be identified as the Supplier that is providing the software at the time of the SBOM creation.
As a minimum expectation, the Supplier Name should be declared for all Components.

However, the Supplier Name for supplied software should be declared differently depending on whether it was incorporated in the Primary Component unmodified or modified from how it was supplied from the upstream Supplier.

If the supplied software package or Component was unmodified when included, the Supplier Name entered is the upstream Supplier’s name with the following guidance: For Suppliers of commercially licensed software, enter the Legal Entity name.
If the Legal Entity name is not globally unique, consider adding the Supplier’s jurisdiction.
In the unlikely case that the Legal Entity name is unknown, consider using the name of the software vendor from https://nvd.nist.gov/products/cpe.
Code: Use vendor name if supplier unknown

Recommendation / Requirement: For Suppliers of open-source software, list the project name.
If known, add the host foundation before the project name (e.g., Apache Tomcat).
Referencing the open-source software (OSS) copyright statements can assist in identifying the supplier(s) or creator(s) of the software.

Although not a recommended practice, if the component’s other attributes uniquely and unambiguously identify the component and the upstream supplier is difficult to identify, either: ■ Enter the domain URL of the software and/or the namespace of the Package URL (PURL). ■ Enter supplier name as unknown.
Code: Use URL or mark supplier unknown

Recommendation / Requirement: If the supplied software package or Component was modified by the Primary Component’s Supplier before being incorporated, enter the Primary Component’s Supplier as the Supplier Name.
Code: Use primary supplier name for modified

Recommendation / Requirement: Additionally, enter the Component’s upstream Supplier in an Attribute field communicating its heritage relationship (e.g., heritage or pedigree).
It is necessary to capture the heritage relationship to enable proper vulnerability monitoring of the Component.
The Originating Supplier, if different from the upstream Supplier, may be a beneficial Attribute to capture for the Component as well.
Both [globally unique or locally unique identifiers] may be declared in the SBOM and provide value.
Minimum Expected - at least one unique identifier should be declared for each Component listed in the SBOM.
Code: Declare at least one unique identifier

Recommendation / Requirement: A globally unique identifier is preferred.
Recommended Practice - list as many globally unique identifiers as available for the Component.
Provide a hash for any Component listed in the SBOM for which the hash was provided or sufficient information is available to generate the hash.
If sufficient information is not available, indicate as unknown.
Code: Indicate hash as unknown if unavailable

Recommendation / Requirement: Along with the hash, provide the hash algorithm and the Component object being hashed to enable reproducibility.
Using a secure hash algorithm is recommended.
Provide at least one hash of the Primary Component at this maturity level.
Along with the minimally expected hash, provide the hash algorithm and the Component object being hashed to enable reproducibility.
If less cryptographically secure, hashes need to be included, adding an additional cryptographically secure hash is required.
Code: Add secure hash if weak hashes used

Recommendation / Requirement: Relationships and relationship completeness declared for the Primary Component and direct Dependencies.
Relationships and relationship completeness declared for all Included Components listed in the SBOM.
Relationships and relationship completeness to as many dynamic and remote Components as possible (e.g., loaded Components or services) are identified.
The choice of direction is not important to the model, as long as one direction is chosen and used consistently.
It is important in this example to maintain the heritage of the modified Component and convey that it has been modified.
Provide license information for the Primary Component.
If the Component’s license is not available in a standard form, refer to the specification for the SBOM format being utilized for alternate methods for declaring the license information.
Provide license information for as many Components as possible.
Code: Provide license for as many components as possible

Recommendation / Requirement: Provide license information for all listed SBOM Components.
Attestation of Concluded License information, i.e., license text and concluded terms and conditions, is included in the SBOM.
Provide copyright notice for the Primary Component.
Provide copyright notice for as many Components as possible.
Code: Provide copyright for as many components as possible

Recommendation / Requirement: Provide copyright notice for all listed SBOM Components.
